title: The Near-Extinction That Created Every Fish
hook_text: ONE fish family won the ocean
script: One group nearly vanished — and somehow ended up owning the entire ocean. Ray-finned fish — actinopterygians — were almost wiped out 252 million years ago during the Permian extinction, when 96% of all marine life died. Only a handful of lineages survived. But here's where it breaks your brain: that near-total collapse forced them to diversify so explosively fast that today they make up 99% of every fish species on Earth. The catastrophe was the engine. Send this to someone who thinks extinction always means the end.
search_summary: Ray-finned fish actinopterygians survived the Permian extinction and now represent 99% of all fish species on Earth.
comment_question: If near-extinction built the ocean's biodiversity, what does that say about us? 🌊
